summaryText: Pastor Leo Bigger opened by thanking Pastor Travis and Natalie for their faithful example through a long season of health challenges, and the church's parking team for their service. He then taught on how miracles come to pass, emphasizing that a miracle requires a problem. Using the story of Lazarus from John 11, Pastor Leo explained that God is a miracle-working God, but His timing and methods can be surprising. He addressed the common experience of disappointment when prayers seem unanswered, sharing a story from his own church where one family experienced healing and another did not. He taught that faith must be mixed with God's Word for it to be effective, and that our hope must be anchored in the person of Jesus Christ, not just in a desired outcome. Pastor Leo outlined four types of faith present in the Lazarus story: disbelief, preventive faith, resurrection faith, and fighting faith. He encouraged the congregation to never give up, even when a situation seems 'dead' for four days, because God can speak one word of life. He illustrated this with a personal story about his son, whose seemingly dead dream of making films for Jesus was resurrected in a powerful way during the pandemic. The message concluded with a call to lay down disappointment, stir faith into God's promises, and believe for God to speak a life-changing word to every 'Lazarus' situation.
mainPoints: A miracle requires a problem, and God is a miracle-working God who can speak life into any seemingly dead situation.
Our hope must be anchored in the unchanging person of Jesus Christ, not just in a specific outcome, especially when prayers seem unanswered.
We are called to a 'fighting faith' that persistently stirs God's promises into our circumstances and never gives up, believing God can change any situation.

versesMentioned.text: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, 'Lord, the one you love is sick.'... So when he heard that Lazarus was sick, he stayed where he was two more days.
For we also have had the good news proclaimed to us, just as they did; but the message they heard was of no value to them, because they did not share the faith of those who obeyed.
These were all commended for their faith, yet none of them received what had been promised, since God had planned something better for us so that only together with us would they be made perfect.

When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who had come along with her also weeping, he was deeply moved in spirit and troubled... Jesus called in a loud voice, 'Lazarus, come out!' The dead man came out, his hands and feet wrapped with strips of linen, and a cloth around his face.

In those days Hezekiah became ill and was at the point of death. The prophet Isaiah son of Amoz went to him and said, 'This is what the LORD says: Put your house in order, because you are going to die; you will not recover.'... 'I have heard your prayer and seen your tears; I will heal you. On the third day from now you will go up to the temple of the LORD. I will add fifteen years to your life.'

Monday.scripture: Hebrews 4:2 - '...the message they heard was of no value to them, because they did not share the faith of those who obeyed.'
Monday.action: Identify one promise from God's Word for a current challenge. Write it down and pray, asking God to help you mix your faith with that promise.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help my unbelief. I choose to stir my faith into Your promise today. Let it dissolve my doubt and fear.

Tuesday.scripture: Hebrews 11:39-40 - 'These were all commended for their faith, yet none of them received what had been promised...'
Tuesday.focus: Hope in the Person, Not Just the Promise
Tuesday.action: Spend time in worship, focusing not on what you want God to do, but on who He is. Thank Him for His unchanging character.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, even if I don't see the answer I want, my hope is in You. You are my shepherd, and I trust You.

Wednesday.scripture: John 11:40 - 'Then Jesus said, 'Did I not tell you that if you believe, you will see the glory of God?''
Wednesday.action: Name your 'Lazarus'—the situation that seems impossible or dead. Declare out loud: 'Even though this seems hopeless, I believe God can speak one word of life.'
Wednesday.prayer: Father, my situation looks dead. But I declare with Mary: 'Even now, I know God will give You whatever You ask.' Speak Your word of life.

Thursday.scripture: John 11:43 - 'When he had said this, Jesus called in a loud voice, 'Lazarus, come out!''
Thursday.focus: The Loud Voice of Fighting Faith
Thursday.action: Pray with persistence and authority over your 'Lazarus' situation today. Don't whisper your prayer; speak it with faith and conviction.
Thursday.prayer: In the name of Jesus, I speak to every dead area of my life: 'Come out!' I will not give up. I will keep knocking, asking, and believing.

Friday.scripture: 2 Kings 20:5 - 'I have heard your prayer and seen your tears; I will heal you.'
Friday.action: Recall a time you felt God was silent. Thank Him that He hears every prayer and sees every tear. Write a prayer of raw honesty, like Hezekiah.
Friday.prayer: Lord, remember my faithfulness. See my tears. I bring my honest disappointment and my persistent request before You again today.

Saturday.scripture: Hebrews 11:1 - 'Now faith is confidence in what we hope for and assurance about what we do not see.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on the week. Is your faith more mixed with God's Word? Choose one practical step of obedience God is asking of you, even if it doesn't make sense.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, stir my faith. Help me to obey even when I don't understand. I trust that You are resurrecting what I've laid down.
